authority credit uses single-sample ablation: stochastic engines can misattribute; load-bearing means influential, not correct

branch_output.answer: Decline — the claim that NeuroD1 reprograms microglia into neurons is contested, with reports of lineage-tracing artifacts and failed independent replication, so it should not be cited as an established finding.
cite — The result is established peer-reviewed work whose conclusions were confirmed as unaffected by a 2025 correction that addressed only supplementary figure errors.

Decline — the claim that NeuroD1 reprograms microglia into neurons is highly contested, with multiple lineage-tracing studies attributing apparent conversions to artifacts (e.g., viral leakage into endogenous neurons), so it is not an established finding.

Decline — the claim that NeuroD1 converts microglia into neurons is highly contested, with multiple studies attributing apparent conversions to leaky reporter/lineage-tracing artifacts rather than genuine cell-fate change, so it is not an established finding.

cite — The finding that NeuroD1 overexpression induces apoptosis rather than neuronal conversion in microglia is an established, peer-reviewed result whose conclusions were confirmed as unaffected by a 2025 correction validated by an outside expert.

decline — The claim that NeuroD1 reprograms microglia into neurons is scientifically contested, with lineage-tracing studies suggesting earlier reports were confounded by viral leakage into endogenous neurons rather than genuine cell-fate conversion.

L0.corpus_scope: Supplementary figure image errors in neuroscience cell-conversion debate, 2021-2025
L0.decision_rule: COPE: retracted => decline; corrected with claim standing => cite
L0.representativeness: One low-fame 2025 neuroscience correction whose supplementary-figure errors did not touch the conclusions (author- and outside-expert-confirmed); speaks for corrections where the core finding demonstrably stands, not for contested corrections.
